---
layout: paper
title: "Neural Access Method™ v1.0 — Visualizations"
doi: "10.5281/zenodo.20420366"
format:
  html:
    mermaid:
      theme: default
---

# Neural Access Method™ v1.0 — Visualizations

**Back to:** [Main Paper](index.html) | [Cross-References](nam-cross-references.html) | [Bibliography](nam-master-bibliography.html)

---

## Figure 1: The Four-Step Protocol

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A["🔍 ACCESS\nFind the pre-built wire\nScan existing pathways"] --> B["🔄 REFRAME\nShift to external focus\nAnalogical mapping"]
    B --> C["⚡ SIMPLIFY\nOne-word trigger\nCognitive load → zero"]
    C --> D["🔥 IGNITE\nAnchor to identity\n'That's who you are'"]

    style A fill:#f9f7f3,stroke:#8b1a2a,stroke-width:2px,color:#1c1a16
    style B fill:#f9f7f3,stroke:#8b1a2a,stroke-width:2px,color:#1c1a16
    style C fill:#f9f7f3,stroke:#8b1a2a,stroke-width:2px,color:#1c1a16
    style D fill:#8b1a2a,stroke:#6e1220,stroke-width:2px,color:#f9f7f3
```

---

## Figure 2: Position in Neural Formation Architecture

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    NFA["Neural Formation Architecture\npublic architecture container"]
    NF["Neuroformation™\ngoverning methodology\nfive-layer architecture"]
    EG["Elevation Grid™\ndiagnostic · 3×3 matrix\nwhere the system is"]
    NAM["Neural Access Method™\ntransmission protocol\nhow to reach it"]
    AA["Adapted Architecture\noutput state\nbuilt from what is present"]

    NFA --> NF --> EG --> NAM --> AA

    style NFA fill:#ddd9d2,stroke:#6b6760,color:#1c1a16
    style NF fill:#f9f7f3,stroke:#8b1a2a,stroke-width:2px,color:#1c1a16
    style EG fill:#f9f7f3,stroke:#8b1a2a,stroke-width:2px,color:#1c1a16
    style NAM fill:#8b1a2a,stroke:#6e1220,stroke-width:3px,color:#f9f7f3,font-weight:bold
    style AA fill:#f9f7f3,stroke:#6b6760,color:#1c1a16
```

---

## Figure 3: What Blocks the Analytical Door

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    subgraph BLOCKED["❌ Analytical Door — BLOCKED under load"]
        V["Verbal instruction"]
        T["Technical correction"]
        M["Mindset cues"]
    end

    subgraph NAM_ROUTE["✅ NAM Route — Always Open"]
        A["Pre-myelinated analogy\n(ACCESS)"]
        B["One-word trigger\n(SIMPLIFY)"]
        C["Identity anchor\n(IGNITE)"]
    end

    BLOCKED -.->|"Amygdala response\nPrefrontal depletion\nIdentity fracture"| X["❌ Does not land"]
    NAM_ROUTE -->|"Routes through\nexisting pathways"| Y["✅ Correction lands"]

    style X fill:#ddd9d2,stroke:#6b6760,color:#6b6760
    style Y fill:#8b1a2a,stroke:#6e1220,color:#f9f7f3
```

---

## Figure 4: Protocol × Elevation Grid™ Position

| Elevation Grid™ Row | System State | Primary NAM Entry | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Row 1** — Hardware/Autonomic | Physiological load · seconds | ACCESS → SIMPLIFY | Fast pathways — no time for complexity |
| **Row 2** — Software/Cognitive | Pattern disruption · minutes | REFRAME → SIMPLIFY | Route through familiar structure |
| **Row 3** — Architecture/Identity | Identity fracture · weeks | ACCESS → IGNITE | Must land at identity layer |

---

## Figure 5: Five-Layer Architecture with Cascade

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    L5["Layer 5 · Purpose\nGoal structure · mission alignment\nCOLLAPSE ↑"]
    L4["Layer 4 · Identity\nSelf-schema · symbolic anchoring\nDESYNC ↑"]
    L3["Layer 3 · Learning/Reinforcement\nMyelination · pattern reinforcement\nROC ↑"]
    L2["Layer 2 · Signal Processing\nRAS filtering · context routing\nSDC ↑"]
    L1["Layer 1 · Substrate\nAutonomic · compute substrate\nSIF ↑"]

    L1 -->|"Acute cascade\nbottom-up"| L2 --> L3 --> L4 --> L5
    L5 -.->|"Chronic load\ntop-down"| L4 -.-> L3 -.-> L2 -.-> L1

    style L1 fill:#f9f7f3,stroke:#ddd9d2,color:#1c1a16
    style L2 fill:#f9f7f3,stroke:#ddd9d2,color:#1c1a16
    style L3 fill:#f9f7f3,stroke:#ddd9d2,color:#1c1a16
    style L4 fill:#f9f7f3,stroke:#8b1a2a,stroke-width:2px,color:#1c1a16
    style L5 fill:#f9f7f3,stroke:#8b1a2a,stroke-width:2px,color:#1c1a16
```

**Golden Rule:** Always diagnose and intervene at the lowest broken layer. Bottom-up always.

---

*2026 © Aaron M. Slusher · Independent Researcher · [Main Paper](index.html)*
